title: Search of citizenship records: After you apply - Canada.ca
content:
## Search of citizenship records: After you apply
- 1. About the process

## Processing your application
We couldn't load the data. Try again. ?
Processing times will vary based on:
- the type of application submitted
- if the application is complete
- how quickly we expect to process applications we’ve already received
- how easily we can verify your information
- how long you take to respond to any requests or concerns
Learn how processing times are calculated .
Processing time is the average for how long it takes from when an application is received to when we make a decision.
Learn more about processing times
Answer a few questions to know
- what happens after you submit your application
- how to update and track your application during processing

## After you applied on paper from Canada or the United States
If you have a tracking number with a postal or courier service, you can check when your application gets delivered to us.
Once we get your application for a search of citizenship records, we’ll check if it’s complete. If it is, we’ll start processing it. We won’t automatically send you a confirmation.

### If your application is incomplete
If something is missing, we’ll return your application and ask for the missing information.

### Track and update your application
During processing, track and update your application by
- checking the status of your application (opens in a new tab)
- telling us if you change your address (opens in a new tab)
- contacting us via the web form (opens in a new tab) to update other information in your application

#### If you need urgent processing
We can process applications faster than our normal processing times in special cases , such as
- confirming to a foreign government that you’re not a citizen For example, to get a passport from that country.
- For example, to get a passport from that country.
- showing you are (or are not) a citizen to get a certain job
- administering the estate of a deceased person or executing a will
To ask for urgent processing of your submitted application
- fill out our web form (opens in a new tab) .
- include your full name, date of birth, email address and telephone numbers a letter explaining why you need urgent processing any documents that support your request for urgent processing Make sure your document is in the correct format .
- your full name, date of birth, email address and telephone numbers
- a letter explaining why you need urgent processing
- any documents that support your request for urgent processing Make sure your document is in the correct format .
- Make sure your document is in the correct format .
We’ll review your request to see if it qualifies. Even if you qualify, we can’t guarantee that you’ll get your search of citizenship record on time.
When you upload files, they must be
- in PDF, JPEG, TIFF, DOC, or DOCX format
If you include multiple files, the total size must be 3.5 MB or smaller.
How to reduce file size (opens in a new tab)
- A letter from a foreign government stating that they need the information.
- Documents that show you’re administering an estate or the executor of a will.
- A letter from an employer.

### Get your search result
We’ll email you a printable PDF letter with the search result.
If you asked for a paper copy, we’ll mail it to your mailing address in Canada or the United States.
- It’s valid for 1 year . If the person whose record you searched for is deceased, the letter doesn’t expire.
- If the person whose record you searched for is deceased, the letter doesn’t expire.
We’ll send you a letter to tell you
- when the person became a Canadian citizen or
- if they have a Canadian citizenship application in progress
We’ll send you a letter to tell you if
- there’s no record or
- we can’t find a record with the information you gave us

## After you apply online
After you submit your online application, you’ll see a confirmation page. This page
- confirms you successfully submitted your application
- has a confirmation number keep it for your records
- keep it for your records
You’ll also automatically get
- an acknowledgement of receipt (AOR) email, and
- an application number (opens in a new tab)

### If your application is incomplete
If something is missing, we’ll
- send a message in your account to ask for the missing information
- cancel your application if you don’t respond by the deadline in the message

### Track and update your application
During processing, track and update your application by
- checking the status of your application (opens in a new tab)
- telling us if you change your address (opens in a new tab)
- contacting us via the web form (opens in a new tab) to update other information in your application

#### If you need urgent processing
We can process applications faster than our normal processing times in special cases , such as
- confirming to a foreign government that you’re not a citizen For example, to get a passport from that country.
- For example, to get a passport from that country.
- showing you are (or are not) a citizen to get a certain job
If you need to ask for urgent processing of an online application you’ve already submitted
- fill out our web form (opens in a new tab)
- include your full name, date of birth, email address and telephone numbers a letter explaining why you need urgent processing any documents that support your request for urgent processing Make sure your documents are in the correct format .
- your full name, date of birth, email address and telephone numbers
- a letter explaining why you need urgent processing
- any documents that support your request for urgent processing Make sure your documents are in the correct format .
- Make sure your documents are in the correct format .
We’ll review your request to see if it qualifies. Even if you qualify, we can’t guarantee that you’ll get your search of citizenship record on time.
- A letter from a foreign government stating that they need the information.
- Documents that show you’re administering an estate or the executor of a will.
- A letter from an employer.
When you upload files, they must be
- in PDF, JPEG, TIFF, DOC, or DOCX format
If you include multiple files, the total size must be 3.5 MB or smaller.
How to reduce file size (opens in a new tab)

### Get your search result
We’ll send a letter with the search result to your online account (opens in a new tab) .
- It will be a PDF you can download and print.
- It’s valid for 1 year . If the person whose record you searched for is deceased, the letter doesn’t expire.
- If the person whose record you searched for is deceased, the letter doesn’t expire.
We’ll send you a letter to tell you
- when the person became a Canadian citizen or
- if they have a Canadian citizenship application in progress
We’ll send you a letter to tell you if
- there’s no record or
- we can’t find a record with the information you gave us

## If you applied from outside Canada and the United States and provided an email
Once we receive your application for a search of citizenship records, we’ll check if it’s complete . If it is, we’ll start processing it. We won’t automatically send you a confirmation.

### If your application is incomplete
We’ll return your application and ask for the missing information.

### Track and update your application
During processing, track and update your application by:
- checking the status of your application (opens in a new tab)
- telling us if you change your address (opens in a new tab)
- contacting the embassy, high commission or consulate (opens in a new tab) where you submitted your application (to update other information)

#### If you need urgent processing
We can process applications faster than our normal processing times in special cases , such as
- confirming to a foreign government that you’re not a citizen (for example, to get a passport from that country)
- administering the estate of a deceased person or executing a will
- showing you are (or are not) a citizen to get a certain job
To ask for urgent processing of your submitted application, contact the Canadian embassy, consulate or high commission (opens in a new tab) where you submitted your application.
We’ll review your request to see if it qualifies. Even if you qualify, we can’t guarantee that you’ll get your search of citizenship record on time.

### Get your search result
We’ll email you a printable PDF.
If you asked for a paper copy, we’ll mail your letter to the embassy, high commission or consulate where you submitted your application.
- It’s valid for 1 year . If the person whose record you searched for is deceased, the letter doesn’t expire.
- If the person whose record you searched for is deceased, the letter doesn’t expire.
We’ll send you a letter to tell you
- when the person became a Canadian citizen or
- if they have a Canadian citizenship application in progress
We’ll send you a letter to tell you if
- there’s no record or
- we can’t find a record with the information you gave us

## If you applied from outside Canada and the United States but didn’t provide an email
Once we receive your application for a search of citizenship records, we’ll check if it’s complete . If it is, we’ll start processing it. We won’t automatically send you a confirmation.
Contact the Canadian embassy, high commission or consulate (opens in a new tab) where you submitted your application to confirm that we received it.

### If your application is incomplete
We’ll return your application and ask for the missing information.

### Track and update your application
During processing, track and update your application by
- checking the status of your application (opens in a new tab)
- telling us if you change your address (opens in a new tab)
- contacting the embassy, high commission or consulate (opens in a new tab) where you submitted your application (to update other information)

#### If you need urgent processing
We can process applications faster than our normal processing times in special cases .
You can ask for urgent processing if you need to
- confirm to a foreign government that you’re not a citizen (for example, to get a passport from that country)
- administer the estate of a deceased person or execute a will
- show you are (or are not) a citizen to get a certain job
To ask for urgent processing of your submitted application , contact the Canadian embassy, consulate or high commission (opens in a new tab) where you submitted your application.
We’ll review your request to see if it qualifies. Even if you qualify, we can’t guarantee that you’ll get your search of citizenship record on time.

### Get your search result
We’ll mail your letter to the embassy, high commission or consulate where you submitted your application.
- It’s valid for 1 year . If the person whose record you searched for is deceased, the letter doesn’t expire.
- If the person whose record you searched for is deceased, the letter doesn’t expire.
We’ll send you a letter to tell you
- when the person became a Canadian citizen or
- if they have a Canadian citizenship application in progress
We’ll send you a letter to tell you if
- there’s no record or
- we can’t find a record with the information you gave us
